question: What are the open issues in remote sensing VSR?

plan.normalized_question: What are the open issues in remote sensing VSR?
sub_goals: surface unresolved limitations and open issues

evidence.title: Open problems in remote sensing video super-resolution
evidence.focus: Summarizes unresolved challenges across degradation realism, alignment robustness, and benchmark design.
evidence.method: Review-style synthesis of common failure modes.
evidence.limitations: The survey highlights problems but does not propose a unified solution.

evidence.title: Benchmarking remote sensing VSR evaluation protocols
evidence.focus: Compares evaluation setups across satellite and aerial video benchmarks.
evidence.method: Standardizes train-test splits, metrics, and downsampling assumptions.
evidence.limitations: Benchmark coverage is still narrow for cloud, haze, and low-light conditions.

evidence.title: Motion-aware degradation modeling for remote sensing VSR
evidence.focus: Jointly models blur, aliasing, jitter, and compression instead of assuming clean bicubic downsampling.
evidence.method: Estimate composite degradation first, then perform temporally aligned restoration.
evidence.limitations: The degradation recipe is still synthetic, so real-world transfer remains weak.

evidence.title: Temporal alignment under satellite viewpoint drift
evidence.focus: Studies temporal alignment failure when camera motion and target motion coexist.
evidence.method: Uses drift-robust temporal fusion before upsampling.
evidence.limitations: Performance drops on long clips with abrupt scene change.

findings: Open problems in remote sensing video super-resolution: Summarizes unresolved challenges across degradation realism, alignment robustness, and benchmark design.
Benchmarking remote sensing VSR evaluation protocols: Compares evaluation setups across satellite and aerial video benchmarks.
Motion-aware degradation modeling for remote sensing VSR: Jointly models blur, aliasing, jitter, and compression instead of assuming clean bicubic downsampling.
Temporal alignment under satellite viewpoint drift: Studies temporal alignment failure when camera motion and target motion coexist.
Most evidence emphasizes degradation realism, temporal alignment, and benchmark comparability as central research bottlenecks.
limitation_signals: The survey highlights problems but does not propose a unified solution.
Benchmark coverage is still narrow for cloud, haze, and low-light conditions.
The degradation recipe is still synthetic, so real-world transfer remains weak.
Performance drops on long clips with abrupt scene change.

critique.review_target: What are the open issues in remote sensing VSR?
blind_spots: Evidence covers the major themes, but unified evaluation protocols and real-world validation remain incomplete.
risks: The sample corpus is illustrative and should be replaced with real paper notes for production use.
A high-level synthesis can still overfit to the retrieved shortlist if recall is poor.
limitation_signals: The survey highlights problems but does not propose a unified solution.
Benchmark coverage is still narrow for cloud, haze, and low-light conditions.
The degradation recipe is still synthetic, so real-world transfer remains weak.
Performance drops on long clips with abrupt scene change.

evaluation.summary: Coverage is broad enough for a first-pass synthesis.
